response:
To find the indefinite integral of the function:

$$
7 \sin(1 - 7x)
$$

we use the substitution method. Let:

$$
u = 1 - 7x
$$

Then:

$$
\frac{du}{dx} = -7 \quad \Rightarrow \quad du = -7 \, dx \quad \Rightarrow \quad dx = -\frac{1}{7} \, du
$$

Now substitute into the integral:

$$
\int 7 \sin(1 - 7x) \, dx = \int 7 \sin(u) \left(-\frac{1}{7}\right) \, du = -\int \sin(u) \, du
$$

Now integrate:

$$
-\int \sin(u) \, du = -(-\cos(u)) + C = \cos(u) + C
$$

Now substitute back $ u = 1 - 7x $:

$$
\cos(1 - 7x) + C
$$

---

### Final Answer:

$$
\boxed{\cos(1 - 7x) + C}
$$
